channel.title: Energy & Environment
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: White House calls oil CEOs as energy prices squeeze voters
item.description: Gasoline prices remain high as the conflict with Iran continues.

media:content.media:title: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, left, and Energy Secretary Chris Wright deliver remarks outside the White House on March 19, 2025, in Washington, D.C.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Oil company founded by energy secretary paid no US taxes last year
item.description: Liberty Energy made $193 million last year and received more than $10 million back in tax benefits, according to a nonprofit analysis released on Tax Day.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright is set to testify on his agency's fiscal 2027 budget request this week.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Soraghan

item.title: Trump’s decision to blockade Iran ups the ante on prices
item.description: Trump’s blockade of Iran is rippling beyond oil, squeezing fertilizer and helium supplies and raising the risk of higher food prices and wider economic disruption.

media:content.media:credit: Mass Communication Specialist 3rd Class Zachary Pearson/U.S. Navy via AP
media:content.media:title: In this Nov. 19, 2019, photo made available by the U.S. Navy, the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln, left, the air-defense destroyer HMS Defender and the guided-missile destroyer USS Farragut transit the Strait of Hormuz with the guided-missile cruiser USS Leyte Gulf.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman and Grace Yarrow
item.dc:contributor: By Scott Waldman and Grace Yarrow

item.title: Missouri town fires half its city council over data center deal
item.description: Residents of a St. Louis suburb turned out in droves to unseat four incumbents just days after the council approved a development agreement for a $6 billion data center.

media:content.media:title: The Festus, Missouri, City Council is getting a makeover after voters last week ousted every incumbent up for reelection. The new council will be sworn in Monday at Festus City Hall (pictured).

item.dc:creator: By Jeff Tomich

item.title: IMF managing director: 'Everybody feels the pinch of prices going up'
item.description: But Kristalina Georgieva said that some countries are being hit much worse than others.

media:content.media:title: "Poor, vulnerable countries," Kristalina Georgieva said, "whether they're in Asia or in Sub-Saharan Africa, they're being hammered dramatically, and when we discuss our response, we will zero in on these highly vulnerable countries."

item.dc:creator: By David Cohen

item.title: Oil industry pleads its Hormuz case with White House
item.description: Oil executives are citing international treaties, sanction laws and high payments in opposing Iran's plan to charge ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

media:content.media:title: Liberia-flagged tanker Shenlong Suezmax, carrying crude oil from Saudi Arabia, is seen at the Mumbai Port in Mumbai, India, on March 12.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and Phelim Kine

item.title: Zeldin stars at climate denial conference
item.description: The EPA administrator became the first agency chief to speak at the Heartland Institute's annual gathering of people who say climate change benefits humanity.

media:content.media:title: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin announces the repeal of the endangerment finding in February.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman and Jean Chemnick
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman and Jean Chemnick

item.title: Wisconsin city passes nation’s first anti-data center referendum
item.description: The vote suggests ballot measures could become a powerful new tool for grassroots activists seeking to block data center projects.

media:content.media:title: A rendering shows the proposed Vantage Data Centers campus in Port Washington, Wisconsin.

item.dc:creator: By Tyler Katzenberger

item.title: Why Hungary’s energy policy is less MAGA than JD Vance might think
item.description: The country’s low power bills are a result of big subsidies.

media:content.media:title: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto welcomes U.S. Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance as they arrive at Budapest Ferenc Liszt International Airport on April 7, 2026 in Budapest, Hungary.

item.dc:creator: By Zia Weise

item.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record with daring moon flyby that included a solar eclipse
item.description: The four astronauts eclipsed the distance record of 248,655 miles set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

media:content.media:title: This image provided by NASA Monday, April 6, 2026, shows the Moon, the near side (the hemisphere we see from Earth) visible at the top half of the disk, identifiable by the dark splotches. At the lower center is Orientale basin, a nearly 600-mile-wide crater that straddles the Moon’s near and far sides. Everything below the crater is the far side. (NASA via AP)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: This Democratic governor is touting her role in the Iran war
item.description: Arizona is facing deep cuts to the water supply that feeds its desert economy. Gov. Katie Hobbs is banking on President Donald Trump to blunt them.

media:content.media:title: Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs speaks during her "Arizona First" rally at El Rio Center on Nov. 1, 2025, in Tucson, Arizona.

item.dc:creator: By Annie Snider

item.title: Artemis II astronauts are more than halfway to the moon as they seek to break Apollo 13’s record
item.description: The crew will travel more than 252,000 miles from Earth – a distance record for humans – before hanging a U-turn behind the moon and heading home.

media:content.media:title: This image from video provided by NASA shows the Artemis II crew Commander Reid Wiseman, second from left, thanking the families of the crew while speaking with NASA Mission Control in a video conference while en route to the moon, Thursday, April 2, 2026, as Canadian astronaut and mission specialist Jeremy Hansen, far left, looks on and mission specialist Christina Koch and pilot Victor Glover, far right, make hearts with their hands. (NASA via AP)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: Trump wants the world to buy more US oil. He might regret it.
item.description: President Donald Trump wants the U.S. to export more oil to make up for Iran war-induced supply crunches.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Claudine Hellmuth/POLITICO (source images via Getty and iStock)
media:content.media:title: EE.OilExplainer.jpg

item.dc:creator: By Mike Soraghan

item.title: Artemis II astronauts rocket toward the moon after spending a day around Earth
item.description: Translunar ignition came 25 hours after launch, setting three Americans and a Canadian on a course for a lunar fly-around on Monday.

media:content.media:title: This image taken from video provided by NASA shows the Earth, left, from NASA's Orion spacecraft as it fired its engines heading toward the moon Thursday, April 2, 2026. (NASA via AP)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: Musk's exploding megarocket puts $8B in space investments at risk
item.description: Dozens of startups working on data centers, mining and pharmaceuticals are hanging on Elon Musk's long-stalled megarocket.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX's megarocket Starship blasts off for a test flight from Starbase, Texas, on Oct. 13, 2025. The rockets have exploded more than a dozen times during testing.

item.dc:creator: By Corbin Hiar

item.title: No one wanted to redevelop this polluted property. Then came AI.
item.description: The Trump administration sees abandoned industrial sites as ripe for building data centers. A Wisconsin city offers an $8 billion test case.

media:content.media:title: A view of the General Motors plant in Janesville, Wisconsin. The site is being considered for redevelopment to house a data center complex.

item.dc:creator: By Miranda Willson

item.title: One casualty of Democrats’ affordability pivot: Climate goals
item.description: The upfront costs of transitioning to clean energy and worries about rising utility bills are forcing tough choices for Democratic leaders.

media:content.media:title: Rhode Island Gov. Dan McKee (D) speaks during a 2021 event at the Statehouse in Providence.

item.dc:creator: By Adam Aton and Marie J. French
item.dc:contributor: By Adam Aton and Marie J. French

item.title: Former Trump Iran adviser says war is headed for escalation
item.description: After accurately forecasting Iran’s response before U.S. strikes, a former White House Iran expert explains why there’s no clear off-ramp — and why escalation may be next.

media:content.media:title: An Iranian civil defense member walks with a hose next to a destroyed fuel tanker vehicle near an ongoing fire following an overnight airstrike on the Shahran oil refinery in northwestern Tehran on March 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Republicans split on how to legislate on data centers
item.description: President Donald Trump wants Congress to act. His party has yet to come up with a unified plan.

media:content.media:title: House Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky., left) and Subcommittee on Energy Chair Bob Latta (R-Ohio) say they don't expect any imminent action on data center legislation.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo and Amelia Davidson
item.dc:contributor: By Nico Portuondo and Amelia Davidson

item.title: Texas saddles up for data center clash
item.description: The energy and tech sectors say data centers can help boost the economy and ease electricity prices — but skepticism is growing across the political spectrum, including in deep-red parts of Texas.

media:content.media:title: An entrance to the Stargate artificial intelligence data center complex in Abilene, Texas, on Monday.

item.dc:creator: By Shelby Webb, Jason Plautz and Kelsey Tamborrino
item.dc:contributor: By Shelby Webb, Jason Plautz and Kelsey Tamborrino

item.title: Trump faces new oil shock threat as Iran eyes second strait
item.description: A major shipping choke point on the Red Sea could come under Iran-sponsored attack to further disrupt global energy supplies.

media:content.media:credit: European Union's Operation Aspides via AP
media:content.media:title: A Greek-flagged oil tanker in the Red Sea under attack by Yemen's Houthi rebels in 2024. Fear is growing that the Iran-backed Houthis could close a crucial strait in the Red Sea if the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran continues.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Ukrainian energy official: Lifting Russia oil sanctions is ‘wrong approach’
item.description: Oleksii Riabchyn, chief international officer at Naftogaz, also described Ukraine's efforts to position itself as a major natural gas hub for Europe.

media:content.media:description: Naftogaz executive talks Ukranian energy potential and impact of Russian attacks
media:content.media:title: Naftogaz executive talks Ukranian energy potential and impact of Russian attacks

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Governors forgo past response to high gasoline prices
item.description: State leaders in both parties say gas tax holidays did little to help consumers the last time a war shook the oil market. They see even less reason to try them now.

media:content.media:title: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis speaks last year during a news conference in Miramar, Florida.

item.dc:creator: By Adam Aton

item.title: US must reopen Strait of Hormuz to cut spiking energy prices, oil chief says
item.description: American Petroleum Institute CEO Mike Sommers says industry leaders want to "make sure that we're finishing the job in Iran."

media:content.media:description: API CEO Mike Sommers talks Iran, state of the U.S. oil industry and more
media:content.media:title: API CEO Mike Sommers talks Iran, state of the U.S. oil industry and more

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: Machado: Venezuela could quintuple oil production under a democratic government
item.description: The Venezuelan opposition leader laid out a detailed plan to oil executives gathered in Houston that would transform Venezuela's oil sector from a state-run enterprise to a 'fully private' industry.

media:content.media:title: Maria Corina Machado said her plan could raise the country’s oil production from the current roughly 1 million barrels a day to as much as 5 million barrels a day, higher than the country produced at its peak.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Burgum: Iran energy price shock a matter of weeks, not months
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum stressed President Donald Trump is 'empathetic' about rising prices.

media:content.media:description: Interior Sec. Burgum: Trump 'empathetic' to rising energy prices
media:content.media:title: Interior Sec. Burgum: Trump 'empathetic' to rising energy prices

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: ‘The worst I’ve seen’: Oil industry grapples with the fallout from US-Israel war with Iran
item.description: The Middle East war is scrambling markets and threatening to remake the energy industry.

media:content.media:title: A speedboat operated by members of Iran's Revolutionary Guard approaches a British-flagged oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz in 2019.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Ending Iran war now would cede Hormuz to the enemy, Trump’s former Defense secretary says
item.description: "You’d see a tax for every ship that goes through" Hormuz if Trump quits now, Gen. Jim Mattis said.

media:content.media:title: The assessment from former Gen. Jim Mattis comes as President Donald Trump declared a five-day pause on military strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure after the U.S. and Israeli attack against Iran enters its fourth week.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: ‘The opportunity is here’: TerraPower CEO credits NRC for moving quickly on projects
item.description: TerraPower recently became the first company to secure a permit from the NRC to construct a full, commercial advanced nuclear reactor.

media:content.media:description: TerraPower CEO says it's 'unfortunate' that nuclear isn't ready to meet AI demand
media:content.media:title: TerraPower CEO says it's 'unfortunate' that nuclear isn't ready to meet AI demand

item.dc:creator: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Francisco "A.J." Camacho
item.dc:contributor: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Francisco "A.J." Camacho

item.title: White House energy adviser says oil companies ‘all on board’ with upping production
item.description: Jarrod Agen, the executive director of the White House National Energy Dominance Council, said he has not received pushback to the administration's calls to drill more amid the war in Iran.

media:content.media:description: Jarrod Agen on Iran strikes: 'President Trump knows exactly what he's doing'
media:content.media:title: Jarrod Agen on Iran strikes: 'President Trump knows exactly what he's doing'

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales
